steps.title: Identify the unifying principle
steps.content: The passage's decisive clue is the final sentence: both of Villasana's approaches—depicting famous people and depicting ordinary people—serve the identical purpose of showing “the power and interconnectedness of all people.”

steps.title: Match the main idea to the choices
steps.content:
Choice C names both types of subjects Villasana depicts (famous and everyday), identifies the themes her works explore (human strength and connection), and reflects what the passage explicitly says her art accomplishes.
The other three either frame her work as a career progression rather than parallel approaches (A), narrow the main idea to a single work with an invented hierarchy of themes (B), or introduce information the passage never provides (D).
That matches choice C — C is correct.
